full_text:
The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated first using Dispase, and then followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H).

Dispase dissociation media included 19.4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 400 µl Dispase + 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 17.8 ml DMEM/F12+++, 2 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through 70 µm cell strainer.

RBC lysis with 4 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ . After RBC lysis and centrifugation, the final pellet was resuspended in 5 ml of media and counted.

Cell counts:

Biobanking

From the dispase-dissociated fraction, 12 cryovials of 7.44E6 cells each were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)

With the objective of isolating as many and as pure fibroblasts as possible, the C&H-dissociated fraction was subjected to further purification using two approaches:

Positive isolation: Enriching the fibroblasts using anti-fibroblasts beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-050-601) and an LS Column (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-042-401) in a QuadroMACS separator (Miltenyi)

Negative isolation: Depleting the sample from cancer and immune cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in a QuadroMACS separator.

For the first approach, the protocol from the anti+fibroblasts microbeads’ datasheet was followed.

For the second approach, both of the protocols from the corresponding beads were used as guides.

In both cases, a 30 um strainer was attached to the corresponding LS column, and samples were filtered through it before being loaded into the columns.

Flow cytometry

From the original cell suspension, 8 aliquots of 1.5 E6 cells each were made in 1.5 mL eppendorfs.

The conditions to stain were:

DCM FMO

unpurified MC

anti-fibroblast column bound

anti-fibroblast flow through

negative isolation column bound

negative isolation flow through

The aliquots were stained with the antibodies from the fibroblast panel according to the protocol.
